one_line_thesis: Aflac is a dual-market supplemental insurer with durable Japan cancer/medical distribution and a U.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Aflac is a dual-market supplemental insurer with durable Japan cancer/medical distribution and a U.S. worksite voluntary-benefits franchise. FY2025 owner cash normalizes to about $5.85 per share (underwriting plus recurring investment spread, between operating cash flow $4.78 and operating income $6.44 per share). Q1 2026 showed revenue of $4.35 billion and net income of $1.02 billion (diluted EPS $1.98), with investment income still moving reported GAAP earnings quarter to quarter. At ~$122 (NYSE, July 2026), the Lawrence base 7-year annual return is about 8% (bear ~5%, bull ~11%), below t...

pricing_analysis.conclusion: Hurdle prices are derived from the decision-grade universal contract; they do not constitute a capital decision.

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

scenario_contract.rule: Cases must change cited causal operating, capital, probability, timing, or financing drivers—not only a terminal multiple.

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: High-return compounder
applicability_tests: Owner earnings can be separated from stock compensation, working capital, and maintenance capital.
Incremental returns on retained capital are observable and durable.
The competitive advantage explains pricing, retention, share, or cost outcomes.
Terminal value is supported by a reinvestment runway rather than an unexplained multiple.
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: The current component schedule is the first decision-grade baseline; attribution begins with the next dated valuation snapshot.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

economic_value.accounting_reference: FY2025 10-K: book value per share $55.13; owner cash $5.85/sh; economic value in normalized supplemental underwriting and investment spread.

economic_claim.description: One diluted share of Aflac, including Japan and U.S. supplemental owner-cash engines, net financial claims, and investment/currency stress reserve.
economic_claim.unit_source: FY2025 weighted average diluted shares 534.878M per AFL/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20260225_rpt20251231_acc0001628280_26_011402.htm.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: Operating segments valued through owner-cash discount paths on premium-proportional owner cash; net debt and stress reserve are separate overlap keys.

timeline.label: Net income moved -33.0% versus the prior period (5,443.0 to 3,646.0).
Basic EPS moved -29.3% versus the prior period (9.7 to 6.8).
Reported FY2025 results: revenue $18.9B, net income $3.65B (investment marks vs prior year), dividend raised to $2.32 per share
Issued yen-denominated senior notes via 424B5 shelf; continued balance-sheet funding for Japan operations and investment portfolio
Raised U.S. dollar senior notes through 424B5; part of recurring debt ladder for capital management
Board and executive changes filed on 8-K; governance refresh alongside Japan and U.S. leadership transitions
Updated U.S. product and distribution strategy filings; continued shift toward hospital indemnity and network dental adjacencies
Post-pandemic Japan sales recovery and U.S. worksite return-to-office distribution normalization after 2020-2021 disruption
Japan FSA solvency and reserving framework continued to govern Aflac Japan capital; U.S. supplemental products remain state-regulated
Multi-year share repurchase and dividend growth program; retained earnings accumulated to $54.7B by FY2025

industry.structure: Supplemental health and life insurance is concentrated among a handful of scaled brands in Japan (third-sector cancer and medical) and U.S. worksite voluntary benefits; pricing is experience-rated with high persistency once policies are sold.
industry.share_shift: Aflac retains leading share in Japan cancer supplemental through agency and bank channels; U.S. share is contested by Unum, MetLife, and regional carriers but Aflac's worksite brand remains entrenched.
industry.trend: Aging demographics support Japan medical riders; U.S. employers continue offering voluntary accident and hospital indemnity as primary medical deductibles rise; investment income sensitivity to rates and yen remains the main swing factor in reported earnings.
